markdownRemark.html:
Basic Configuration

Regardless of whether virtual scrolling mode is enabled, the component library supports customizing the loading animation effect through spin.

Non-Virtual Scrolling Mode

By using the spin property of the Table, you can customize the Table's loading effect. For a demo, refer to Table Spin Controlled.

Custom Loading Indicator with indicator

Through indicator, you can pass a custom rendered Spin DOM element. The loading effect style needs to be implemented customly. For example, the Spin effect on the official website:

The code is as follows:

const c7nIcon = (
  <span className="custom-spin-dot-test">
    <i />
    <i />
    <i />
    <i />
  </span>
);
<Table spin={{indicator: c7nIcon, spinnig: true}} dataSet={this.userDs} />
.custom-spin-dot-test {
  transform: rotate(45deg);
  animation: c7nRotateTest 1.2s infinite linear;
}
.custom-spin-dot-test i {
  width: 45%;
  height: 45%;
  border-radius: 100%;
  background-color: #3f51b5;
  transform: scale(.75);
  display: block;
  position: absolute;
  opacity: .3;
  animation: c7nSpinMoveTest 1s infinite linear alternate;
  transform-origin: 50% 50%;
}
.custom-spin-dot-test i:nth-child(1) {
  left: 0;
  top: 0;
}
.custom-spin-dot-test i:nth-child(2) {
  right: 0;
  top: 0;
  animation-delay: .4s;
}
.custom-spin-dot-test i:nth-child(3) {
  right: 0;
  bottom: 0;
  animation-delay: .8s;
}
.custom-spin-dot-test i:nth-child(4) {
  left: 0;
  bottom: 0;
  animation-delay: 1.2s;
}
@keyframes c7nSpinMoveTest {
  to {
    opacity: 1;
  }
}
@keyframes c7nRotateTest {
  to {
    transform: rotate(405deg);
  }
}

spinning display state customization

The Table defaults to the Spin loading state, which by default is associated with DataSet.status. When status is loading, a loading indicator will be shown.

Users can customize the display and hiding of the loading indicator through the spinning property. This can be done by associating with a DataSet or by complete self-control.

Examples are as follows:

// Method 1: Fully customize control through spinning
<Table dataSet={this.ds} spin={{indicator: c7nIcon, spinning: this.state.spinning}}/>
// Method 2: Control through associating with DataSet
<Table dataSet={this.ds} spin={{indicator: c7nIcon, dataSet: this.spinDs}/>

Related API

For detailed instructions, please see Spin API and Table Spin API.

Virtual Scrolling Mode

In virtual scrolling mode, Spin is not enabled by default. This mode also supports custom spin, implemented in the same way as non-virtual scrolling.

If this effect is needed, you can enable the loading icon during virtual scrolling through virtualSpin. Once enabled, the spin loading effect will also appear while scrolling.

Related API

All APIs for non-virtual scrolling are compatible in virtual scrolling mode.

Precautions

To avoid loading lag issues when dealing with large datasets, try to avoid using performance-intensive properties such as stroke-dasharray.

Common Issues

Loading Lag When Loading Large Amounts of Data

Symptoms

When a Table handles a large amount of data, during its initial screen load, the loading may freeze, only displaying a small portion of the arcs. Additionally, once the Table has finished loading, the full display (360°) will only be shown at the end.

Reason

  1. The Spin component in the component library contains an animation that uses stroke-dasharray (this style itself has performance issues: https://bugs.chromium.org/p/chromium/issues/detail?id=167569).
  2. JS execution is single-threaded. With large amounts of data, the browser's rendering capability is limited. When handling a Table with a lot of data, it can cause issues with the animation display. The small arc segments that appear are the starting frame effect (0%) of the stroke-dasharray property in the animation.

Solutions

Solution 1: Custom Table Spin

Core idea: Customize the Spin component to avoid using the stroke-dasharray style in the animation.

Essentially, it avoids using the stroke-dasharray-related styles in the library's Spin component. For reference, see Controlled Table Spin.

Note: The animation in the custom Spin must also not include stroke-dasharray.

Solution 2: Override Spin Default Styles

Core idea: Leverage the initial frame characteristic of the animation during stutter to override the animation styles of the library's Spin that are related to stroke-dasharray.

Effect:

Note: If the UED dependency package in the project environment is version 4.x, you need to pay attention to the priority of style overrides.

.c7n-pro-table {
  &-wrapper {
    .c7n-spin-nested-loading .c7n-spin-dot {
      animation: c7nTableDefaultSpinRotate 1.4s linear infinite;
    }
    .c7n-progress-loading .c7n-progress-inner svg {
      animation: c7nTableDefaultSpinRotate 1.4s linear infinite;
    }
    .c7n-progress-loading .c7n-progress-inner circle {
      animation: c7nTableDefaultSpinLoading 1.4s ease-in-out infinite;
    }
    @keyframes c7nTableDefaultSpinRotate {
      0% {
        transform: rotate(0deg);
      }
      100% {
        transform: rotate(360deg);
      }
    }

    @keyframes c7nTableDefaultSpinLoading {
      0% {
        stroke-dasharray: 100px 200px;
        stroke-dashoffset: 0;
      }
      30% {
        stroke-dasharray: 100px 200px;
        stroke-dashoffset: -15px;
      }
      100% {
        stroke-dasharray: 100px 200px;
        stroke-dashoffset: -120px;
      }
    }
  }
}
